rxn_class_summary: Visible-light photocatalytic dearomative cascade of 1-naphthol arenes bearing tethered alkenes, giving either [2+2] cycloadducts or benzocyclobutene products via catalyst-controlled sequential triplet energy transfer

key_intermediates.name: Ir(III) triplet excited state photocatalyst (3*Ir(III), T1)
Triplet styrene 2a* (second EnT-activated intermediate, selective with PC III)
1,n-Diradical intermediate 4 from vinylcyclobutane C-C bond homolysis
Benzocyclobutene product 3a (after ISC and radical-radical recombination)

electron_flow: Blue-light excites Ir(III) photocatalyst to its triplet state, which transfers triplet energy to naphthol 1a giving 1a*; intramolecular [2+2] cycloaddition with the pendant alkene yields styrene 2a. With PC III (higher ET), a second EnT to 2a generates triplet 2a*, which undergoes vinylcyclobutane ring-opening to diradical 4 that ISCs and recombines to benzocyclobutene 3a.
rate_determining_step: Second energy transfer from 3*Ir(III) to styrene 2a (slow with PC II, fast with PC III), governing divergence between 2a and 3a

probe_reagents.name: Stern-Volmer quencher 1a (naphthol substrate)
probe_reagents.observation: Both PC II and PC III are efficiently quenched by 1a (linear S-V), supporting EnT activation of 1a

probe_reagents.name: Stern-Volmer quencher 2a (styrene intermediate)
probe_reagents.observation: Only PC III is effectively quenched by 2a; PC II shows poor quenching, rationalizing catalyst-controlled divergence

probe_reagents.name: Thermal (dark) control
probe_reagents.observation: No reaction in absence of light/photocatalyst, precluding a thermal nonphotocatalytic pathway

probe_reagents.name: Reaction monitoring by 1H NMR
probe_reagents.observation: With PC II, 1a converts cleanly to 2a; with PC III, 2a forms transiently then converts to 3a, consistent with sequential EnT

notes: DFT-computed triplet energies (ET) and redox potentials of 1a (ET=59.8 kcal/mol) and 2a (ET=55.9 kcal/mol) combined with catalyst ET values (PC I 57.8, PC II 49.2, PC III 60.1 kcal/mol) rationalize catalyst-selective EnT. Crystal structures of 2a and 3a confirm connectivity/stereochemistry. The benzocyclobutene-forming step is proposed via a triplet vinylcyclobutane rearrangement (biradical 4) followed by ISC; an alternative concerted di-π-methane-like pathway is not explicitly invoked. No reaction without a carbonyl EWG on the naphthol, presumably needed to lower arene triplet energy.
